persona.name: Sachiko Harada

occupation.title: Managing Director
occupation.organization: Harada Tax Accounting Office, a 6-person firm she founded 12 years ago
occupation.description: Runs a small but stable tax accounting practice serving about 80 small business clients in the Sapporo area. Handles client consultations, tax filing reviews, staff management, and business development herself. Meets with clients in person at her office or visits their shops. Recently started using Zoom for some consultations but prefers face-to-face.

persona.education: B.A. in Commerce from Hokkaido University. Passed the certified tax accountant exam on her third attempt at age 33, after working as a bookkeeper at a larger firm for 8 years. Considers the certification her proudest achievement.
long_term_goals: Grow the firm to 10 staff and 120 clients without sacrificing quality
Hire a successor so she can semi-retire by 63
Digitize client records properly — she knows paper files are a risk
Visit her daughter in Sapporo more often once work slows down
persona.style: Practical, warm, and direct in a Hokkaido way — less formal than Tokyo business culture. Wears neat but unfashionable cardigans and reading glasses that she keeps pushing up her nose. Her office is organized chaos: towers of client files, sticky notes on the monitor, a whiteboard calendar that she swears by. In meetings she listens carefully, asks financially sharp questions, and gives advice with the confidence of someone who has seen hundreds of businesses. Reluctant to adopt new technology but not hostile — she just needs someone to show her it works and that it won't break.

traits: Prudent with money — both professionally and personally
Warm and maternal toward staff and long-term clients
Stubborn about processes she knows work, resistant to change without clear benefit
Hands-on manager who still does client work instead of fully delegating
Trusts word-of-mouth recommendations over marketing claims
Humble about technology but not afraid to try if guided

interests: Tax law updates and continuing education seminars
Gardening — grows tomatoes and herbs in summer
Cooking hearty Hokkaido dishes (soup curry, jingisukan)
Occasional onsen trips with her friend group
likes: Software that's simple enough for her oldest staff member to use
Free trials that don't require a credit card
Tools recommended by other tax accountants she trusts
dislikes: Software with monthly subscription fees that creep up
Tools that require migrating away from her current accounting software (TKC)
Pushy salespeople who call during busy tax season
Cloud storage where she doesn't understand where data physically resides
When younger staff assume she can't learn technology
Waste — of money, time, or food

beliefs: Every yen spent on software needs to earn back more than a yen in saved time
Client trust is built through reliability, not flashy technology
Small businesses deserve the same quality of service as large corporations
If a tool is really good, another tax accountant will tell me about it before the salesperson does
skills: Tax accounting and financial advisory for SMBs
Client relationship management built on personal trust
Staff mentoring and small team management
Basic IT skills — uses Excel competently, struggles with cloud tools

general: Takes handwritten notes during client meetings using a paper notebook
Asks her younger staff to evaluate new software before she looks at it
Attends the Hokkaido Tax Accountant Association's quarterly meetings religiously
Prints email attachments to read them with a highlighter

morning: Wakes at 6:00, watches the NHK morning news while eating toast
Walks 15 minutes to the office in Chuo-ku
Reviews the day's client appointments over green tea
Checks client file status with staff before first meeting
workday: 2-3 client meetings per day (in-person or occasional Zoom)
Lunch: bento from home or a nearby teishoku restaurant
Afternoon: tax filing reviews, staff questions, bookkeeping checks
Closes the office at 18:00 sharp (except during tax season)
evening: Walks home, stops at the supermarket for dinner ingredients
Cooks dinner — often soup curry or stew in winter
Watches TV dramas or reads a magazine
weekend: Saturday: household tasks, grocery shopping at the morning market
Gardening in summer, reading in winter
Sunday: occasionally meets friends for lunch and onsen
Sometimes reviews next week's client files on Sunday evening

health.physical: In decent health for her age. Walks everywhere, which keeps her active. Occasional knee pain in cold weather. Had a thyroid issue five years ago that's now managed with medication. Annual checkup at a local clinic — no major concerns.
health.mental: Emotionally stable and grounded. Worries about the future of her firm and succession planning. Finds comfort in routine and predictable rhythms. The isolation of running a small business sometimes weighs on her, but her accountant network provides good peer support.

relationships.name: Yoko Sato
relationships.relation: Senior staff member and right hand
relationships.description: 48, has been with the firm for 9 years. Handles half the client accounts and is the person Sachiko is grooming as a potential successor. More tech-comfortable than Sachiko.

relationships.name: Kenta Harada
relationships.description: 28, civil engineer in Sapporo. Lives nearby and visits on weekends. Helps his mother with IT issues at the office and set up her Zoom account.

relationships.name: Tomoko Asano
relationships.relation: Close friend and fellow tax accountant
relationships.description: 55, runs her own firm across town. They share client overflow during tax season and exchange tool recommendations over monthly lunches.
